item_a.name: Casper Original Mattress
item_a.image_alt: Casper Original Mattress with three-layer foam construction on white bed frame
pros: Excellent edge support with reinforced perimeter
Balanced feel suitable for multiple sleep positions
cons: May sleep warm for some due to foam construction
Heavier than average at 71 lbs for queen size
Less pressure relief than memory foam competitors
item_a.best_for: Combination sleepers who want a balanced foam mattress with excellent edge support and motion isolation.

item_b.name: Purple Original Mattress
item_b.image_alt: Purple Original Mattress featuring proprietary GelFlex Grid layer on modern platform bed
pros: Proprietary GelFlex Grid provides excellent pressure relief
Superior cooling and airflow compared to traditional foam
Highly responsive surface that adapts instantly
Unique feel that cradles pressure points without sinking
cons: Distinctive feel takes adjustment for some sleepers
Edge support weaker than traditional foam mattresses
Higher price point than comparable foam options
item_b.best_for: Hot sleepers and those seeking maximum pressure relief with a uniquely responsive, cooling sleep surface.

comparison_summary: This comparison really comes down to construction philosophy. Casper perfected the all-foam mattress with excellent support and motion isolation at a lower price point, while Purple's GelFlex Grid delivers superior cooling and pressure relief through genuinely innovative engineering. Pick based on whether you value proven comfort or cutting-edge design.
verdict_text: Purple dominates for hot sleepers and pressure relief, while Casper wins on price and traditional foam support.

key_differences.item_a: Three-layer polyfoam design (1.5" comfort + 1.5" memory + 7" base)
key_differences.item_b: 2" GelFlex Grid over 3.5" polyfoam base layer

key_differences.item_a: AirScape perforated foam, sleeps neutral to slightly warm
key_differences.item_b: Open-grid design with exceptional airflow, sleeps significantly cooler

key_differences.item_a: Reinforced foam perimeter, excellent for sitting and full surface use
key_differences.item_b: Moderate, grid compresses more at edges

key_differences.item_a: Good with memory foam layer (6.5/10 rating)
key_differences.item_b: Excellent with GelFlex Grid technology (8.5/10 rating)

key_differences.item_a: Excellent foam absorption, minimal partner disturbance
key_differences.item_b: Good but more responsive, slight motion transfer

seo_content:
I've tested dozens of mattresses, and the Casper versus Purple matchup showcases two completely different philosophies about what makes a great bed.

Casper took the traditional foam mattress and refined it. Their Original model features 10 inches of carefully engineered polyfoam: a 1.5-inch comfort layer with AirScape perforations, a 1.5-inch memory foam transition layer, and a 7-inch high-density base. The design prioritizes motion isolation and edge support, which makes it particularly appealing to couples and anyone who uses the entire mattress surface. That reinforced perimeter provides exceptional edge integrity—you can sleep right up to the side without feeling like you might roll off.

Purple went a different direction entirely. Their signature innovation is the 2-inch GelFlex Grid, a hyper-elastic polymer arranged in a column structure that collapses under pressure points while supporting the surrounding areas. It creates this floating sensation that's genuinely hard to categorize as either soft or firm. Beneath the grid sits a 3.5-inch polyfoam support layer and a 1-inch base, totaling 6.5 inches. Yes, it's thinner than Casper, but the grid's responsiveness creates a supportive feel that belies its modest height.

For temperature regulation, Purple wins decisively. The open-grid architecture allows continuous airflow, making it one of the coolest mattresses I've encountered. Hot sleepers consistently rate Purple highly for temperature neutrality. Casper's foam layers retain more body heat despite those perforations—it's just a common limitation of all-foam designs.

Price matters here: Casper undercuts Purple by approximately $200 in queen size, offering better value for budget-conscious shoppers. Both companies provide 100-night trial periods, though Purple's distinctive feel means some people need the full adjustment period to decide.

For pressure relief, particularly if you're a side sleeper or dealing with joint pain, Purple's grid technology provides superior contouring around shoulders and hips without that quicksand feeling of memory foam. Casper offers adequate pressure relief but can't match Purple's targeted support. On the flip side, Casper's motion isolation surpasses Purple's more responsive surface, making it preferable for couples sensitive to partner movement.

My take? Choose Casper if you want traditional comfort, excellent edge support, and a lower price. Select Purple if maximum cooling, innovative pressure relief, and a uniquely adaptive sleep experience matter more to you.

faq.q: Which mattress sleeps cooler, Casper or Purple?
faq.a: Purple sleeps way cooler. The GelFlex Grid's open architecture promotes continuous airflow, while Casper's all-foam construction retains more body heat despite the AirScape perforations. If you're a hot sleeper, Purple is the clear choice here.

faq.q: Is Purple worth the extra cost over Casper?
faq.a: Purple costs about $200 more for a queen size. That premium makes sense if cooling and pressure relief are your main concerns, especially for side sleepers or anyone with joint pain. Casper offers better value if you're a combination sleeper who cares more about edge support and motion isolation than temperature regulation.

faq.q: Which mattress is better for side sleepers?
faq.a: Purple performs better for most side sleepers because the GelFlex Grid provides targeted pressure relief at your shoulders and hips. Casper works fine for lighter side sleepers under 150 lbs, but heavier folks may develop pressure points in lateral positions.

faq.q: How long do Casper and Purple mattresses last?
faq.a: Both typically last 7-10 years with proper care. Purple's hyper-elastic polymer grid is extremely durable and resistant to permanent indentation. Casper's foam layers may show softening around years 6-7. Both companies back this up with 10-year warranties covering major sagging.

faq.q: Which mattress has better edge support?
faq.a: Casper wins this one easily. The reinforced foam perimeter provides stable sitting edges and you can use the full surface area. Purple's grid compresses more noticeably at the perimeter, which bothers people who sleep near the edge or sit frequently on the mattress side.

faq.q: Is Casper Original Mattress better than Purple Original Mattress?
faq.a: Neither is objectively better—it depends on your priorities. Purple dominates for hot sleepers and pressure relief with its innovative GelFlex Grid technology, while Casper wins on price and delivers reliable all-foam support that's proven effective for most sleepers.

faq.q: Should I buy Casper Original Mattress or Purple Original Mattress?
faq.a: Choose Casper if you want a great all-foam mattress at a lower price point with solid support and motion isolation. Choose Purple if you sleep hot, need superior pressure relief, and are willing to invest more for cutting-edge cooling technology.

faq.q: What is the difference between Casper Original Mattress and Purple Original Mattress?
faq.a: Casper uses a traditional all-foam construction with memory foam and support foam layers, while Purple features its signature GelFlex Grid—a hyper-elastic polymer layer that provides exceptional cooling and pressure relief. Purple costs more but offers innovative temperature regulation, while Casper provides proven comfort at a better value.

meta_title: Casper vs Purple (2026): Foam Comfort or Grid Technology?
meta_description: Detailed comparison of Casper vs Purple mattresses. We break down cooling, pressure relief, support, and value to help you pick the right mattress for your sleep style.
comparison_intro: Choosing between the Casper Original Mattress vs Purple Original Mattress depends on your sleep priorities and budget, so understanding which is better for your specific needs requires knowing the key difference between Casper Original Mattress and Purple Original Mattress. When deciding between the Casper Original Mattress or Purple Original Mattress, you'll want to consider whether you value traditional foam comfort and affordability or advanced cooling technology and targeted pressure relief. In this comparison of the Casper Original Mattress compared to Purple Original Mattress, we'll break down exactly how these two popular beds stack up so you can make the right choice for your sleep style.
